label: Package C Resource Metrics Smoke
commands: for i in 1 2 3; do echo "CHAIN_SYNC_RESPONDER_ITERATION=$i"; /usr/bin/time -v cargo test -p amaru-protocols chainsync::responder::tests::intersect -- --nocapture; done 2>&1 | tee package-c-logs/${STAMP}-resource-chainsync-responder-loop.log

for i in 1 2 3; do echo "TRACK_PEERS_INTERSECT_ITERATION=$i"; /usr/bin/time -v cargo test -p amaru-consensus track_peers::tests::test_intersect -- --nocapture; done 2>&1 | tee package-c-logs/${STAMP}-resource-track-peers-loop.log
for i in 1 2 3; do echo "FETCH_BLOCKS_ITERATION=$i"; /usr/bin/time -v cargo test -p amaru-consensus fetch_blocks -- --nocapture; done 2>&1 | tee package-c-logs/${STAMP}-resource-fetch-blocks-loop.log

for i in 1 2 3; do echo "BLOCKFETCH_PROTOCOL_ITERATION=$i"; /usr/bin/time -v cargo test -p amaru-protocols blockfetch -- --nocapture; done 2>&1 | tee package-c-logs/${STAMP}-resource-blockfetch-loop.log
limitations: Bounded resource metrics only; this is not live adversarial load testing.
Uses existing safe unit and stage tests rather than a malicious multi-peer harness.
Does not prove numeric adequacy for MAX_FETCHED_BLOCKS, timeout duration, queue bounds, or retry policy.
Does not execute restart, fork manipulation, persistence mutation, DB corruption, or snapshot deletion.
No public Cardano network is contacted.
safety_notes: Runs inside /opt/dwarf/targets/amaru on the Linux target host.
Captures /usr/bin/time -v metrics for repeated safe test filters.
Evidence remains candidate-only and does not promote risk rows.
